summary_one_line: Input must be a well-formed font whose table directory contains a 'morx' AAT table so apply_morx is true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font blob with a valid magic/sfnt header and a table-directory record carrying the literal four-byte tag 'morx' (0x6d 0x6f 0x72 0x78), so _hb_apply_morx returns true and apply_morx is set; the true side at line 95 then also needs shaper != _hb_ot_shaper_default.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seed ae73a8c9 begins with the 'OTTO' sfnt magic and embeds the exact ASCII bytes 'morx' at offset 0x0c-0x0f, satisfying the morx-table tag match that drives apply_morx true; the lineage of that seed contains an I2S-floor dash row, evidence the morx tag was placed by an input-to-state substitution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking value_profile seeds carry no 'morx' tag at the table-directory offsets (e.g. seed 00546490 holds 'y-hant-hk' / jy text instead), so apply_morx stays false and the true side never fires; loser took the false side 18 times vs winner's 2 true.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which copies a compared constant (the 'morx' tag bytes harfbuzz checks against the directory record) directly into the input offset, planting the 4-byte tag in one shot; value_profile, lacking I2SRandReplace, can only nudge bytes via havoc against a 4-byte exact-equality string compare and effectively never assembles the tag.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the morx-and-non-default-shaper guard at hb-ot-shape.cc:95 and the globally blocked side is the true branch.
apply_morx is computed from _hb_apply_morx over the face, i.e. presence of the AAT morx table.
The winner-resolving seed contains the OTTO sfnt magic and the literal ASCII 'morx' tag bytes.
The winner resolved the branch 8/10 trials while the loser blocked 10/10.
A loser-blocking value_profile seed carries text bytes (jy-hant-hk) rather than a morx font-table tag at those offsets.
On the blocker line the loser takes the false side 18 times and the true side 0, while the winner takes the true side twice.
value_profile_cmplog's mutator set adds I2SRandReplace over value_profile's havoc/token stack.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5692 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed ae73a8c9233c3cb8` and observed one I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 18 of the 19-level ancestor chain (ancestor e0a5b09f80625fb6), consistent with an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on apply_morx where value_profile resolves the true side as fast as value_profile_cmplog despite the morx tag being checked by a 4-byte exact string compare, showing CMP_MAP gradient alone suffices and I2S substitution is not the divergence driver.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner seed shows the clean 'morx' tag; the second winner seed (47909b1d) shows 'mort'/'GPOS' tags, so the exact tag the I2S substitution planted to flip apply_morx is inferred rather than uniquely pinned.
